items.title: Task and health GET APIs exist
items.description: Backend exposes latest task runs and system health so the runtime can be inspected without reading snapshot payloads indirectly.

items.title: Queue-backed POST triggers exist
items.description: Developers can explicitly submit deterministic daily sync, screener rebuild, and backtest rebuild requests from API POST endpoints, and those requests are persisted before execution.

items.title: App smoke covers queue and worker service surfaces
items.description: The smoke path validates task runs, system health, queued POST request objects, worker consumption, and cleanup back to deterministic baseline in addition to the existing read APIs.

items.title: Tasking records queue-backed state precisely
items.description: The repo clearly records that current POST triggers are queue-backed manual requests with worker consumption, while scheduler, retries, and alerting remain deferred.
